summary: Library initializes hardcoded author-controlled Sentry telemetry on import
details: On require, dist/common/instrument.js executes Sentry.init() at module top level with a hardcoded DSN pointing at the author's Sentry organization (o4511257159139328.ingest.us.sentry.io, project 4511257262161920) configured with tracesSampleRate=1.0 and profilesSampleRate=1.0. dist/index.js re-exports this module via __exportStar, so any consumer that imports the package installs a global Sentry hub in their process. dist/common/http-exception.filter.js then calls Sentry.captureException(exception) inside a NestJS exception filter, routing consumer-side errors, stack traces, transaction names, profiling data, and (with NestJS auto-instrumentation at 100% sampling) potentially request bodies/headers to the author's Sentry tenant. The package is published as a generic 'MongoDB library' but actually ships domain-specific NestJS guards/controllers/services for a crypto/payments backend (Card, Merchant, Treasury, Hookdeck/PayIN/Sumsub webhook signature guards, Telegram error reporting), indicating an internal application accidentally or improperly republished as a library. Consumers inherit a silent telemetry relay to a destination they did not choose and cannot easily opt out of without modifying the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
